title: andropogon sanguineus var. oligostachyus
intro: Andropogon sanguineus var. oligostachyus, also known as red grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, fever, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Andropogon sanguineus var. oligostachyus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, wounds, and menstrual disorders.
